scenarios.scenarioName: Adding a contact creates a person entity with fields

assertionResults.description: A person entity should be created for Sarah
Email field should be set correctly

scenarios.responseText:
Done, sir. Agent has created a new contact — **Sarah Chen** — with email `sarah@acme.com` and company **Acme Corp** duly noted on her record.

Should Agent also create a separate Company entry for Acme Corp, or perhaps add a role/title for Ms. Chen?

scenarios.scenarioName: Phaibel can identify the model it is using

assertionResults.description: Response should mention the model name (claude)

scenarios.responseText:
A most reasonable inquiry, sir. Agent is currently conversing with you via **Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 4.6** — a rather capable model, if Agent may say so.

For completeness, here is how the various capabilities are assigned:

| Capability | Model |
|---|---|
| Chat & Reasoning | Claude Sonnet 4.6 |
| Categorise, Summarise & Format | Claude Haiku 4.5 |

The lighter duties — categorising, summarising, formatting — are handled efficiently by the swifter Haiku model, while the more demanding work of reasoning and conversation falls to Sonnet. A sensible division of labour, Agent thinks.
